verses.text: When Rehoboam was established and strong, he forsook the law of the LORD, and all Israel with him. Strength became his snare. Prosperity is a dangerous season, for the heart that needed God in weakness too often forgets Him in success.

Because they transgressed, Shishak of Egypt comes up with a vast army and takes the fortified cities of Judah. The fenced walls fail when God withdraws. No defence stands for a people who have abandoned their true defence.

Because they transgressed, Shishak of Egypt comes up with a vast army and takes the fortified cities of Judah. The fenced walls fail when God withdraws. No defence stands for a people who have abandoned their true defence.

Because they transgressed, Shishak of Egypt comes up with a vast army and takes the fortified cities of Judah. The fenced walls fail when God withdraws. No defence stands for a people who have abandoned their true defence.

Shemaiah tells them, 'Ye have forsaken me, therefore I have left you'; and the princes and king humble themselves, confessing, 'The LORD is righteous.' Judgment brings them low. The first step back to God is to own that His chastening is just.

Shemaiah tells them, 'Ye have forsaken me, therefore I have left you'; and the princes and king humble themselves, confessing, 'The LORD is righteous.' Judgment brings them low. The first step back to God is to own that His chastening is just.

Seeing them humbled, God grants some deliverance, yet makes them Shishak's servants, that they may learn the difference between serving Him and serving the kingdoms of the world. Mercy tempers the rod. God lets us taste bondage to teach how good His own service is.

Seeing them humbled, God grants some deliverance, yet makes them Shishak's servants, that they may learn the difference between serving Him and serving the kingdoms of the world. Mercy tempers the rod. God lets us taste bondage to teach how good His own service is.

Shishak carries off the temple treasures and the golden shields, and Rehoboam replaces them with bronze. Glory is exchanged for a cheaper imitation. Sin always trades gold for brass, and the loss it brings is seldom fully restored.

Shishak carries off the temple treasures and the golden shields, and Rehoboam replaces them with bronze. Glory is exchanged for a cheaper imitation. Sin always trades gold for brass, and the loss it brings is seldom fully restored.

Shishak carries off the temple treasures and the golden shields, and Rehoboam replaces them with bronze. Glory is exchanged for a cheaper imitation. Sin always trades gold for brass, and the loss it brings is seldom fully restored.

Because he humbled himself, the LORD's anger turns away and does not destroy him utterly, and things go well in Judah. Humility stays the stroke. God never despises a broken and contrite heart, but answers it with returning favour.

Rehoboam reigns seventeen years but did evil, 'because he prepared not his heart to seek the LORD'; then he dies. His epitaph names the root of his failure. A heart not set to seek God drifts, however long the reign or fair the start.
